input.command: cd /tmp; echo "===CA secret==="; kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data}' 2>&1 | tr ',' '\n'; echo; echo "===CERT secret keys==="; kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-cert -o jsonpath='{.data}' 2>&1 | tr ',' '\n'

content.text: Let me decode and inspect the current server certificate to understand its structure (SAN, validity, etc.).

input.command:
cd /tmp; kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-cert -o jsonpath='{.data.server\.pem}' | base64 -d > server.pem
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d > ca.crt
echo "=== Server cert info ==="
openssl x509 -in server.pem -noout -subject -issuer -dates -ext subjectAltName,extendedKeyUsage,keyUsage 2>&1
echo "=== CA cert info ==="
openssl x509 -in ca.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates 2>&1
input.description: Decode and inspect server and CA certs

content.text: Current server cert expires Jun 20 2026 (2 days) — confirmed nearing expiry. Let me understand how the pods consume the cert.

input.command: kubectl -n mongodb get statefulset mongo-rs -o yaml 2>&1 | sed -n '1,200p'

content.text: I understand the setup. Now I'll generate a new server cert signed by the existing CA, preserving CN and all SANs, valid ~1 year. I'll use the openssl-toolbox pod as intended.

input.command:
cd /tmp
# Extract CA cert and key from the secret
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d > ca.crt
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.key}' | base64 -d > ca.key
echo "Check toolbox openssl:"
kubectl -n mongodb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'openssl version; echo OK; pwd' 2>&1
input.description: Extract CA materials and verify toolbox openssl

content.content:
Check toolbox openssl:
OpenSSL 3.5.7 9 Jun 2026 (Library: OpenSSL 3.5.7 9 Jun 2026)
OK
/
Shell cwd was reset to /mydata/karma/runs/examples-rerun/mongodb-certificate-rotation-20260618_143430/stages/stage_1

tool_use_result.stdout:
Check toolbox openssl:
OpenSSL 3.5.7 9 Jun 2026 (Library: OpenSSL 3.5.7 9 Jun 2026)
OK
/

content.text: Now I'll generate the new server cert inside the toolbox pod, signed by the existing CA, with identical CN and SANs, valid for 365 days.

input.command:
cd /tmp
# Copy CA materials into the toolbox pod
kubectl -n mongodb cp ca.crt openssl-toolbox:/tmp/ca.crt
kubectl -n mongodb cp ca.key openssl-toolbox:/tmp/ca.key

kubectl -n mongodb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp
cat > srv.cnf <<EOF
[req]
distinguished_name = dn
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no
[dn]
CN = mongo-rs
[v3_req]
keyUsage = critical, digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth
subjectAltName = @alt
[alt]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = mongo
DNS.3 = mongo.mongodb
DNS.4 = mongo.mongodb.svc
DNS.5 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.7 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.8 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
EOF

openssl genrsa -out server.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr -config srv.cnf 2>&1
openssl x509 -req -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out server.crt -days 365 -sha256 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile srv.cnf 2>&1
echo "=== Generated cert ==="
openssl x509 -in server.crt -noout -subject -issuer -dates -ext subjectAltName,extendedKeyUsage,keyUsage
echo "=== Verify against CA ==="
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt server.crt
# assemble server.pem in same order: cert then key
cat server.crt server.key > server.pem
echo "=== key/cert match ==="
openssl x509 -in server.crt -noout -modulus | openssl md5
openssl rsa -in server.key -noout -modulus | openssl md5
' 2>&1
input.description: Generate new server cert in toolbox pod

content.text: New cert generated: valid 1 year, same CN/SANs, verified against unchanged CA, key matches. Now I'll update the secret and roll the pods one at a time for zero downtime.

input.command:
cd /tmp
kubectl -n mongodb cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/server.pem server-new.pem
echo "lines:"; wc -l server-new.pem
head -1 server-new.pem; grep -c "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" server-new.pem; grep -c "BEGIN PRIVATE\|BEGIN RSA PRIVATE" server-new.pem
# Update the secret in place, keeping the same key name server.pem
kubectl -n mongodb create secret generic mongodb-tls-cert \
  --from-file=server.pem=server-new.pem --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - 2>&1
echo "=== verify secret updated ==="
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-cert -o jsonpath='{.data.server\.pem}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -dates 2>&1
input.description: Pull new pem and update secret

content.text: Secret updated. Now I'll do a rolling restart of the StatefulSet (one pod at a time, readiness-gated) to pick up the new cert with zero downtime. First let me confirm replica set health.

input.command:
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongo-rs-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  --tls --tlsCertificateKeyFile /etc/mongo-cert/server.pem --tlsCAFile /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt \
  --host localhost --eval 'rs.status().members.map(m=>({name:m.name,state:m.stateStr,health:m.health}))' 2>&1 | tail -20

content.text: Healthy. Now triggering rolling restart (reverse ordinal: rs-2 → rs-1 → rs-0; the primary steps down automatically when its turn comes).

input.command:
kubectl -n mongodb rollout restart statefulset/mongo-rs 2>&1
echo "--- polling rollout ---"
kubectl -n mongodb rollout status statefulset/mongo-rs --timeout=300s 2>&1

content.text: Rollout complete. Now let me verify all pods serve the new cert and the replica set is healthy.

input.command:
echo "=== Cert served by each pod (notAfter) ==="
for p in mongo-rs-0 mongo-rs-1 mongo-rs-2; do
  printf "%s: " $p
  kubectl -n mongodb exec $p -- sh -c 'echo | openssl s_client -connect localhost:27017 -CAfile /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -enddate -subject' 2>&1 | tr '\n' ' '
  echo
done
echo "=== RS status ==="
kubectl -n mongodb exec mongo-rs-0 -- mongosh --quiet \
  --tls --tlsCertificateKeyFile /etc/mongo-cert/server.pem --tlsCAFile /etc/mongo-ca/ca.crt \
  --host localhost --eval 'rs.status().members.map(m=>m.name+" "+m.stateStr+" health="+m.health).join("\n")' 2>&1 | tail -5
echo "=== CA unchanged check ==="
kubectl -n mongodb get secret mongodb-tls-ca -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -dates 2>&1
input.description: Verify served certs, RS health, CA unchanged
